sentences.quote: We present the first formal verification of the lattice chirality problem in a proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present the first formal verification of the lattice chirality problem in a proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority claim ('first formal verification'); not mechanically verifiable. INFO per spec.

sentences.quote: The nine conditions of the Golterman-Shamir (GS) no-go theorem---six explicit and three implicit---are formalized as substantive Lean~4 propositions using Mathlib infrastructure.
sentences.quote_normalized: the nine conditions of the golterman-shamir (gs) no-go theorem---six explicit and three implicit---are formalized as substantive lean~4 propositions using mathlib infrastructure.
sentences.agent_notes: GoltermanShamir.lean codifies 6 explicit + 3 implicit + 9 total. Matches paper.

sentences.quote: Seven of nine conditions encode genuine mathematical content: translation invariance via Brillouin zone compactness, fermion-only fields via \texttt{ExteriorAlgebra} as the fermionic Fock space (finite-dimensionality proved by the Aristotle automated theorem prover via graded decomposition), relativistic spectrum via spectral gap, and Hamiltonian evolution via matrix exponential.
sentences.quote_normalized: seven of nine conditions encode genuine mathematical content: translation invariance via brillouin zone compactness, fermion-only fields via exterioralgebra as the fermionic fock space (finite-dimensionality proved by the aristotle automated theorem prover via graded decomposition), relativistic spectrum via spectral gap, and hamiltonian evolution via matrix exponential.
sentences.agent_notes: Substantive 7-of-9 count corresponds to substantive_condition_count in GoltermanShamir.lean. fock_space_finite_dim is unnamed Module.Finite instance proved by Aristotle run_20260328_170451.

sentences.quote: The remaining two encode physics conjectures as well-typed axioms.
sentences.quote_normalized: the remaining two encode physics conjectures as well-typed axioms.
sentences.agent_notes: Original phrasing calls fields_complete (a Prop struct field) plus an older gs_nogo_axiom 'axioms'. Current chirality module state: 0 active axiom keywords; gs_nogo_axiom was removed (per source comment). 'Two physics conjectures encoded as axioms' is methodological framing — defensible but loose. WARN.

sentences.quote: The Thorngren-Preskill-Fidkowski (TPF) construction is shown to violate five of the nine conditions: smoothness (round function discontinuous at $1/2$), fermion-only (bosonic rotor ancillas), finite local dimension ($\ell^2(\mathbb{Z})$ proved infinite-dimensional), extra-dimensional (4+1D SPT slab), and conditionally, no massless bosons.
sentences.quote_normalized: the thorngren-preskill-fidkowski (tpf) construction is shown to violate five of the nine conditions: smoothness (round function discontinuous at $1/2$), fermion-only (bosonic rotor ancillas), finite local dimension ($\ell^2(\mathbb{z})$ proved infinite-dimensional), extra-dimensional (4+1d spt slab), and conditionally, no massless bosons.
sentences.agent_notes: Five-violation count matches potential_violation_count in TPFEvasion.lean (3 definite + 2 conditional = 5).

sentences.quote: The master synthesis \texttt{tpf\_outside\_gs\_scope\_main} is a machine-checked five-part conjunction.
sentences.quote_normalized: the master synthesis tpf\_outside\_gs\_scope\_main is a machine-checked five-part conjunction.

sentences.quote: The formalization comprises 54 theorems across three Lean modules (\texttt{LatticeHamiltonian.lean}, \texttt{GoltermanShamir.lean}, \texttt{TPFEvasion.lean}), with zero \texttt{sorry}, verified by \texttt{lake build}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization comprises 54 theorems across three lean modules (latticehamiltonian.lean, goltermanshamir.lean, tpfevasion.lean), with zero sorry, verified by lake build.
sentences.agent_notes: Strict theorem count: 28 (LH) + 14 (GS) + 12 (TPF) = 54. Exact match. Zero sorry confirmed: project-wide grep finds 2 occurrences both inside comment blocks.

sentences.quote: Of these, 14 are proved by the Aristotle automated theorem prover across 4 runs.
sentences.quote_normalized: of these, 14 are proved by the aristotle automated theorem prover across 4 runs.
sentences.agent_notes: 13 chirality Aristotle entries map via lean_deps + 1 unnamed instance fock_space_finite_dim = 14. Distinct run_ids: run_20260328_132925, run_20260328_142342, run_20260328_151228, run_20260328_170451.

sentences.quote: This work provides machine-checked evidence that the GS no-go and TPF construction operate in genuinely different mathematical frameworks---neither proves nor disproves the other.
sentences.quote_normalized: this work provides machine-checked evidence that the gs no-go and tpf construction operate in genuinely different mathematical frameworks---neither proves nor disproves the other.

sentences.quote: A companion paper~\cite{Paper8} extends this analysis with the Gioia-Thorngren positive construction ($[H,Q_A]=0$ machine-verified), Onsager algebra formalization, and $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ anomaly classification, totaling 2237+~theorems (1~axiom: gapped interface) across 130~modules.
sentences.quote_normalized: a companion paper~ extends this analysis with the gioia-thorngren positive construction ($[h,q_a]=0$ machine-verified), onsager algebra formalization, and $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ anomaly classification, totaling 2237+~theorems (1~axiom: gapped interface) across 130~modules.
sentences.agent_notes: IA/SD: paper claims 2237+ theorems / 130 modules. Live: 2578 theorems (within '+' bound) / 170 modules (30.8% drift, '130' is a strict integer -> FAIL). 1 axiom claim matches (gapped_interface_axiom).

sentences.quote: The question of whether chiral fermions can be consistently placed on a lattice---with the correct continuum limit reproducing the Standard Model's chiral gauge structure---remains one of the central open problems in lattice field theory~\cite{Nielsen1981a,Nielsen1981b}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the question of whether chiral fermions can be consistently placed on a lattice---with the correct continuum limit reproducing the standard model's chiral gauge structure---remains one of the central open problems in lattice field theory~.
sentences.agent_notes: Cites Nielsen1981a/b; not in CITATION_REGISTRY (foundational classics, bibitem entries provided). WARN — registry coverage gap.

sentences.quote: Two recent lines of work have produced seemingly contradictory conclusions.
sentences.quote_normalized: two recent lines of work have produced seemingly contradictory conclusions.

sentences.quote: Golterman and Shamir~\cite{GoltermanShamir2026} proved a no-go theorem: under nine conditions (six explicit, three implicit), any lattice Hamiltonian produces a vector-like (equal left- and right-handed) fermion spectrum.
sentences.quote_normalized: golterman and shamir~ proved a no-go theorem: under nine conditions (six explicit, three implicit), any lattice hamiltonian produces a vector-like (equal left- and right-handed) fermion spectrum.
sentences.agent_notes: GoltermanShamir2026 IS in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: Independently, Thorngren, Preskill, and Fidkowski~\cite{TPF2024,Tong2022} constructed a disentangler using bosonic rotor degrees of freedom, an infinite-dimensional local Hilbert space, and a 4+1-dimensional SPT slab, achieving a lattice system with a chiral continuum limit.
sentences.quote_normalized: independently, thorngren, preskill, and fidkowski~ constructed a disentangler using bosonic rotor degrees of freedom, an infinite-dimensional local hilbert space, and a 4+1-dimensional spt slab, achieving a lattice system with a chiral continuum limit.
sentences.agent_notes: Cites TPF2024, Tong2022; neither key in CITATION_REGISTRY. Bibitem block has arXiv IDs. WARN — registry coverage gap.

sentences.quote: These results have been debated informally, with no direct engagement between the two frameworks.
sentences.quote_normalized: these results have been debated informally, with no direct engagement between the two frameworks.

sentences.quote: We provide the first machine-checked formal analysis, formalizing both sides in Lean~4 with the Mathlib library and identifying precisely which GS conditions the TPF construction violates.
sentences.quote_normalized: we provide the first machine-checked formal analysis, formalizing both sides in lean~4 with the mathlib library and identifying precisely which gs conditions the tpf construction violates.
sentences.agent_notes: Self-referential framing.

sentences.quote: The GS no-go theorem assumes six explicit conditions on the lattice Hamiltonian H.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gs no-go theorem assumes six explicit conditions on the lattice hamiltonian h.

sentences.quote: C1. Lattice translation invariance. H(k) is a continuous function on the Brillouin zone $T^d$ with continuous first derivative. Formalized via \texttt{BrillouinZone d := Fin d $\to$ AddCircle ($2\pi$)}, proved compact (\texttt{Pi.compactSpace} + \texttt{AddCircle.compact}).
sentences.quote_normalized: c1. lattice translation invariance. h(k) is a continuous function on the brillouin zone $t^d$ with continuous first derivative. formalized via brillouinzone d := fin d $\to$ addcircle ($2\pi$), proved compact (pi.compactspace + addcircle.compact).

sentences.quote: C2. Fermion-fields-only Hamiltonian. No scalar, bosonic, or ancilla fields. The local Hilbert space is a fermionic Fock space. Formalized as \texttt{FermionicFockSpace k := ExteriorAlgebra $\mathbb{C}$ (Fin k $\to$ $\mathbb{C}$)}, with \texttt{fock\_space\_finite\_dim} proving \texttt{Module.Finite $\mathbb{C}$ (FermionicFockSpace k)} via three Aristotle-discovered helper lemmas: \texttt{exteriorPower\_eq\_bot\_of\_lt}, \texttt{exteriorPower\_iSup\_range}, \texttt{exteriorPower\_sup\_fg} (Aristotle run \texttt{run\_20260328\_170451}).
sentences.quote_normalized: c2. fermion-fields-only hamiltonian. no scalar, bosonic, or ancilla fields. the local hilbert space is a fermionic fock space. formalized as \texttt{fermionicfockspace k := exterioralgebra $\mathbb{c}$ (fin k $\to$ $\mathbb{c}$)}, with fock\_space\_finite\_dim proving \texttt{module.finite $\mathbb{c}$ (fermionicfockspace k)} via three aristotle-discovered helper lemmas: exteriorpower\_eq\_bot\_of\_lt, exteriorpower\_isup\_range, exteriorpower\_sup\_fg (aristotle run run\_20260328\_170451).
sentences.agent_notes: FermionicFockSpace + fock_space_finite_dim resolved. exteriorPower_* are private lemmas at GoltermanShamir.lean lines 188/199/252 (private not exported in lean_deps but verified by source grep). ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS["fock_space_finite_dim"] = run_20260328_170451: match.

sentences.quote: C3. Relativistic continuum limit with no massless bosons. Formalized via \texttt{GaplessPoint} (spectral gap vanishes at isolated momenta) and \texttt{C3\_relativistic} requiring finitely many gapless points with linear dispersion.
sentences.quote_normalized: c3. relativistic continuum limit with no massless bosons. formalized via gaplesspoint (spectral gap vanishes at isolated momenta) and c3\_relativistic requiring finitely many gapless points with linear dispersion.
sentences.agent_notes: Both names exist in module GoltermanShamir; paper uses bare \texttt without module qualifier so resolution succeeds.

sentences.quote: C4. Complete set of interpolating fields. Formalized using resolvent infrastructure: \texttt{C4\_complete} requires \texttt{everywhere\_invertible} (no propagator poles away from gapless points). The physics content (bound-state completeness) is axiomatized via \texttt{fields\_complete}.
sentences.quote_normalized: c4. complete set of interpolating fields. formalized using resolvent infrastructure: c4\_complete requires everywhere\_invertible (no propagator poles away from gapless points). the physics content (bound-state completeness) is axiomatized via fields\_complete.
sentences.agent_notes: everywhere_invertible and fields_complete resolved as nested under C4_complete in lean_deps. fields_complete is a struct field of type Prop (line 324 GS.lean) — paper accurately calls it "axiomatized".

sentences.quote: C5. No spontaneous symmetry breaking. Formalized via \texttt{C5\_no\_ssb} with ground state index, ground state invariance under the symmetry group, and eigenvalue properties.
sentences.quote_normalized: c5. no spontaneous symmetry breaking. formalized via c5\_no\_ssb with ground state index, ground state invariance under the symmetry group, and eigenvalue properties.

sentences.quote: C6. Propagator zeros are kinematical. Formalized as a well-typed existential over basis enlargements that remove propagator zeros.
sentences.quote_normalized: c6. propagator zeros are kinematical. formalized as a well-typed existential over basis enlargements that remove propagator zeros.
sentences.agent_notes: Paper does not name a specific Lean theorem; methodologically descriptive.

sentences.quote: I1. Hamiltonian formulation. Formalized via \texttt{I1\_hamiltonian} requiring a Hermitian generator H with \texttt{H.IsHermitian}.
sentences.quote_normalized: i1. hamiltonian formulation. formalized via i1\_hamiltonian requiring a hermitian generator h with h.ishermitian.

sentences.quote: I2. Local (finite-range) interactions. Formalized via \texttt{FiniteRange}. Proved to follow from C1: \texttt{c1\_implies\_i2} (Aristotle run \texttt{run\_20260328\_142342}).
sentences.quote_normalized: i2. local (finite-range) interactions. formalized via finiterange. proved to follow from c1: c1\_implies\_i2 (aristotle run run\_20260328\_142342).
sentences.agent_notes: ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS["c1_implies_i2"] = run_20260328_142342. Match.

sentences.quote: I3. Finite-dimensional local Hilbert space. The key condition that the TPF construction violates. Proved that the rotor Hilbert space $\ell^2(\mathbb{Z})$ is not finite-dimensional: \texttt{rotor\_hilbert\_not\_finite\_dim} via linear independence of \texttt{lp.single} elements (Aristotle run \texttt{run\_20260328\_132925}).
sentences.quote_normalized: i3. finite-dimensional local hilbert space. the key condition that the tpf construction violates. proved that the rotor hilbert space $\ell^2(\mathbb{z})$ is not finite-dimensional: rotor\_hilbert\_not\_finite\_dim via linear independence of lp.single elements (aristotle run run\_20260328\_132925).
sentences.agent_notes: ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS["rotor_hilbert_not_finite_dim"] = run_20260328_132925. Match.

sentences.quote: The TPF construction violates five GS conditions.
sentences.quote_normalized: the tpf construction violates five gs conditions.

sentences.quote: C1 (smoothness): The disentangler uses the round function to map continuous variables to integers. Proved discontinuous at 1/2: \texttt{round\_not\_continuous\_at\_half} via $\varepsilon$-$\delta$ argument (Aristotle run \texttt{run\_20260328\_132925}).
sentences.quote_normalized: c1 (smoothness): the disentangler uses the round function to map continuous variables to integers. proved discontinuous at 1/2: round\_not\_continuous\_at\_half via $\varepsilon$-$\delta$ argument (aristotle run run\_20260328\_132925).

sentences.quote: I3 (finite-dim): $\ell^2(\mathbb{Z})$ is countably infinite-dimensional. Proved: \texttt{rotor\_hilbert\_not\_finite\_dim}. Also: $\mathbb{Z}$ is infinite (\texttt{integers\_not\_finite}, Aristotle).
sentences.quote_normalized: i3 (finite-dim): $\ell^2(\mathbb{z})$ is countably infinite-dimensional. proved: rotor\_hilbert\_not\_finite\_dim. also: $\mathbb{z}$ is infinite (integers\_not\_finite, aristotle).

sentences.quote: The formal analysis reveals that the GS no-go and TPF construction operate in genuinely different mathematical frameworks.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formal analysis reveals that the gs no-go and tpf construction operate in genuinely different mathematical frameworks.

sentences.quote: The GS theorem assumes a finite-dimensional, purely fermionic, lattice-translation-invariant Hamiltonian. The TPF construction uses infinite-dimensional bosonic rotors, a discontinuous disentangler, and an extra spatial dimension. Neither framework contains the other: the GS no-go does not apply to the TPF construction (which violates its hypotheses), and the TPF construction does not disprove the GS no-go (which remains valid within its scope).
sentences.quote_normalized: the gs theorem assumes a finite-dimensional, purely fermionic, lattice-translation-invariant hamiltonian. the tpf construction uses infinite-dimensional bosonic rotors, a discontinuous disentangler, and an extra spatial dimension. neither framework contains the other: the gs no-go does not apply to the tpf construction (which violates its hypotheses), and the tpf construction does not disprove the gs no-go (which remains valid within its scope).
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive synthesis spanning GS+TPF.

sentences.quote: This is the first formal verification result in the lattice chirality literature.
sentences.quote_normalized: this is the first formal verification result in the lattice chirality literature.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority claim. Cannot mechanically verify "first".

sentences.quote: The machine-checked nature of the analysis eliminates the possibility of subtle errors in the logical structure, which is particularly valuable for a debate where the two sides have not directly engaged.
sentences.quote_normalized: the machine-checked nature of the analysis eliminates the possibility of subtle errors in the logical structure, which is particularly valuable for a debate where the two sides have not directly engaged.

sentences.quote: This work is part of a broader program formalizing the three-layer architecture for emergent physics: Layer~1 (topological/categorical) → Layer~2 (gauge theory) → Layer~3 (EFT/hydrodynamics).
sentences.quote_normalized: this work is part of a broader program formalizing the three-layer architecture for emergent physics: layer~1 (topological/categorical) → layer~2 (gauge theory) → layer~3 (eft/hydrodynamics).

sentences.quote: The chirality wall---whether chiral gauge structure can emerge from a lattice---is one of three structural walls that constrain emergent gravity programs. The formal analysis presented here informs the assessment of whether and how these walls might be breached.
sentences.quote_normalized: the chirality wall---whether chiral gauge structure can emerge from a lattice---is one of three structural walls that constrain emergent gravity programs. the formal analysis presented here informs the assessment of whether and how these walls might be breached.

sentences.quote: The broader formalization program has also produced the first-ever definitions of pivotal, spherical, and fusion categories in a proof assistant, along with the first formalization of the Drinfeld double D(G) and the gauge emergence theorem $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G) \cong \mathrm{Rep}(D(G))$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the broader formalization program has also produced the first-ever definitions of pivotal, spherical, and fusion categories in a proof assistant, along with the first formalization of the drinfeld double d(g) and the gauge emergence theorem $z(vec_g) \cong rep(d(g))$.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority claim ("first-ever"). Lean modules SphericalCategory, FusionCategory, DrinfeldDouble exist. INFO.

sentences.quote: First, the Gioia-Thorngren construction~\cite{GioiaTh2026} is formalized: the central theorem $[H_{\mathrm{BdG}}(\mathbf{k}), q_A(\mathbf{k})]=0$ is machine-verified for all momenta on any finite lattice, with the chiral charge proved non-on-site and non-compact---violating exactly the GS conditions identified here.

sentences.quote_normalized: together, the two papers provide a three-pillar formal verification of the chirality wall: no-go (this work), positive construction, and anomaly classification~.

sentences.quote: Proofs were assisted by the Aristotle automated theorem prover~\cite{Aristotle} (Harmonic, Inc.) which filled 14 of the 54 sorry gaps in the chirality modules, including the key \texttt{fock\_space\_finite\_dim} result via graded decomposition of the exterior algebra.
